article_title: SuperCDMS Dark Matter Detector Reaches Near-Absolute-Zero Operating Temperature at SNOLAB, Preparing for First Science Run

content_preview.title: SuperCDMS Dark Matter Detector Reaches Near-Absolute-Zero Operating Temperature at SNOLAB, Preparing for First Science Run
content_preview.summary: The SuperCDMS experiment at SNOLAB has cooled its 24 detectors to near absolute zero, preparing to probe an unexplored low-mass dark matter range within the year.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The Super Cryogenic Dark Matter Search (SuperCDMS) experiment has reached its target operating temperature of approximately 15 to 30 millikelvins, roughly 100 times colder than the vacuum of outer space, according to the [Department of Energy's SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory](https://www6.slac.stanford.edu/news/2026-03-17-supercdms-cools-down-near-absolute-zero-setting-stage-one-worlds-most-sensitive), which leads the 24-institution international collaboration.
